<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# Agent Working Memory — Food Delivery Platform

This file is the agent's live scratch-pad for a single coding session.
Reset or clear completed sections between tasks. Never commit real secrets here.

---

## 1. Session State

```yaml
session_state:
  task_id: ""                      # Short identifier for the current task, e.g. "add-driver-rating-endpoint"
  task_description: ""             # One-sentence summary of what the agent is trying to accomplish
  status: idle                     # idle | in_progress | blocked | awaiting_review | complete
  phase: ""                        # planning | scaffolding | implementing | testing | refactoring | done
  active_service: ""               # Which microservice is being touched: api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service
  active_language: ""              # Java | Kotlin | Python | Go | TypeScript — must match active_service toolchain
  active_build_system: ""          # maven | gradle | pip | go_mod | npm — must match active_service
  entry_point_in_scope: ""         # e.g. "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/OrderServiceApplication.java"
  target_test_command: ""          # e.g. "cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress"
  started_at: ""                   # ISO-8601 timestamp when this task began
  last_updated_at: ""              # ISO-8601 timestamp of most recent agent turn
  blocking_reason: ""              # Populated only when status == blocked; describes what is stopping progress
  cross_service_impact: false      # true if changes touch more than one service — triggers extra review caution
  openapi_change_required: false   # true if the REST contract in openapi.yaml must change (requires human approval)
```

---

## 2. Decisions Made

```yaml
decisions_made:
  # One entry per meaningful technical decision. Append; never delete.
  - id: ""                         # Unique short slug, e.g. "use-redis-lock-for-stock"
    timestamp: ""                  # ISO-8601
    service: ""                    # Affected service(s), e.g. "inventory-service"
    component: ""                  # Class/file/function most affected, e.g. "inventory-service/main.py::reserve_stock"
    decision: ""                   # What was decided, stated plainly
    rationale: ""                  # WHY: tradeoffs considered, alternatives rejected
    alternatives_rejected: []      # List of options that were NOT chosen and why each was dropped
    constraints_respected:         # Which project constraints drove the decision
      forbidden_ops_avoided: []    # Items from agent_forbidden_operations that were consciously avoided
      pitfalls_avoided: []         # Items from potential_pitfalls that were consciously avoided
    follow_up_required: false      # true if this decision needs a later migration, cleanup, or human sign-off
    follow_up_note: ""             # What that follow-up is
```

---

## 3. Files Modified

```yaml
files_modified:
  # One entry per file touched in this session. Append on every write.
  - path: ""                       # Repo-relative path, e.g. "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
    service: ""                    # Which microservice owns this file
    language: ""                   # Java | Kotlin | Python | Go | TypeScript
    change_type: ""                # created | modified | deleted
    reason: ""                     # Why this file was changed (ties back to task_description)
    jpa_entity_touched: false      # true if the file is or modifies a JPA @Entity (Order, OrderItem) — migration may be needed
    schema_migration_needed: false # true if a DB schema change is implied (PostgreSQL for order/inventory, MongoDB for driver)
    migration_script_path: ""      # Path to the migration file if schema_migration_needed == true
    rabbitmq_contract_touched: false  # true if event payload shape or exchange name "order.events" is affected
    rest_contract_touched: false   # true if a REST endpoint signature changed (coordinate with openapi.yaml)
    auth_header_touched: false     # true if X-Customer-Id header handling or JWT logic was modified
    redis_lock_touched: false      # true if acquire_lock / stock_quantity logic in inventory-service was modified
    stripe_logic_touched: false    # true if PaymentIntent capture, cancel, or refund logic was modified
    order_status_touched: false    # true if OrderStatus enum or isValidTransition was modified
    test_file: false               # true if this is a test file (not production code)
    verified_by_test: false        # true if a passing test covers this change
    restricted_path_risk: false    # true if path is in restricted_write_paths — should never be true; flag for review
```

---

## 4. Tests Run

```yaml
tests_run:
  # One entry per test execution. Append; do not overwrite previous runs.
  - run_id: ""                     # Unique short ID, e.g. "run-001"
    timestamp: ""                  # ISO-8601
    service: ""                    # order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service | api-gateway
    framework: ""                  # JUnit | pytest | go_test | Jest
    command: ""                    # Exact command executed, e.g. "cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress"
    scope: ""                      # unit | integration | e2e | all
    triggered_by: ""               # Why the run was initiated, e.g. "after modifying OrderService.java"
    result: ""                     # passed | failed | errored | skipped
    tests_total: 0
    tests_passed: 0
    tests_failed: 0
    tests_skipped: 0
    coverage_pct: null             # Numeric percentage if reported, else null
    failed_test_names: []          # List of failing test method names, e.g. ["OrderServiceTest::testCancelOrder"]
    failure_summary: ""            # Human-readable summary of why failures occurred
    order_status_transitions_covered: []   # OrderStatus transitions exercised, e.g. ["PENDING->ACCEPTED", "ACCEPTED->CANCELLED"]
    stripe_mocked: null            # true | false | null — whether Stripe SDK was mocked in this run
    rabbitmq_mocked: null          # true | false | null — whether RabbitMQ was mocked
    redis_mocked: null             # true | false | null — whether Redis was mocked
    test_properties_file: ""       # e.g. "order-service/src/test/resources/application-test.properties" (read-only)
```

---

## 5. Open Questions

```yaml
open_questions:
  # Items that require human clarification, approval, or external input before the agent can proceed safely.
  - id: ""                         # Short slug, e.g. "q-stripe-capture-timing"
    raised_at: ""                  # ISO-8601
    raised_during_phase: ""        # Which session phase surfaced this question
    service_affected: ""           # Which service(s) this blocks
    question: ""                   # The specific question needing an answer
    context: ""                    # Why this matters — what decision it unblocks
    urgency: ""                    # blocking | high | medium | low
    # blocking  → agent cannot proceed on this task without an answer
    # high      → agent can continue on other parts but this must be resolved before PR
    # medium    → should be answered before merging
    # low       → nice-to-have clarification, agent has made a reasonable assumption
    assumption_if_unblocked: ""    # If urgency != blocking, what assumption the agent made to continue
    resolved: false                # Flip to true once answered
    resolution: ""                 # The answer or decision once resolved
    resolved_at: ""                # ISO-8601
```

---

## 6. Domain State

```yaml
domain_state:
  # Tracks live knowledge about domain concepts and framework state accumulated during the session.

  order_lifecycle:
    last_known_status_graph:       # Mirrors isValidTransition logic — update if OrderStatus.java is changed
      PENDING:      [ACCEPTED, CANCELLED]
      ACCEPTED:     [PREPARING, CANCELLED]
      PREPARING:    [READY_FOR_PICKUP]
      READY_FOR_PICKUP: [IN_TRANSIT]
      IN_TRANSIT:   [DELIVERED]
      DELIVERED:    []
      CANCELLED:    [REFUNDED]
      REFUNDED:     []
    status_values_in_codebase: []  # Full list of enum values confirmed present in OrderStatus.java
    illegal_transitions_attempted: []  # Any transitions that raised IllegalStateException during this session

  stripe:
    payment_intent_capture_mode: MANUAL   # Confirmed from codebase — do not change without human approval
    open_payment_intents: []       # payment_intent_ids created but not yet captured or cancelled this session
    refunds_issued: []             # payment_intent_ids for which a refund was triggered this session

  jpa_and_schema:
    entities_modified: []          # List of @Entity classes touched, e.g. ["Order", "OrderItem"]
    element_collections_touched: false   # true if @ElementCollection on Order (order_items table) was modified
    pending_migrations: []         # Descriptions of schema changes not yet scripted
    datasource_in_use: ""          # Confirmed value of SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL for current run context

  inventory_service:
    async_session_confirmed: true  # Reminder: all DB calls must use AsyncSession + await (asyncpg driver)
    redis_lock_pattern: "acquire_lock / release"   # Canonical lock function names in main.py
    stock_reservation_items: []    # MenuItem IDs for which stock was reserved this session
    unlimited_stock_sentinel: -1   # stock_quantity == -1 means unlimited; never lock these items

  rabbitmq:
    exchange_name: "order.events"  # FROZEN — changing this breaks notification-service and any other consumers
    events_published_this_session: []   # List of event type strings emitted, e.g. ["order.created", "order.cancelled"]
    events_consumed_this_session: []    # Events consumed by notification-service or other consumers this session

  api_gateway:
    auth_header_forwarded: "X-Customer-Id"   # Header name injected by JwtAuthFilter — do not rename
    jwt_claim_for_customer: "sub"            # JWT claim used to extract customer ID
    routes_modified: []            # List of route IDs changed in application.yml this session

  driver_service:
    routing_style: "manual switch/case"   # No router framework — edits must follow existing pattern in main.go
    db_backend: "MongoDB"
    location_struct: "GeoPoint"           # Struct holding lat/lng in MongoDB documents

  notification_service:
    idempotency_store: "in-memory Set"    # Known limitation — does not survive restarts; Redis replacement is a future task
    sms_provider: "Twilio"
    email_provider: "SendGrid"
    events_handled: []                    # RabbitMQ event types this service is confirmed to handle

  cross_cutting:
    services_touched_this_session: []    # Names of services modified; if > 1, cross_service_impact should be true
    env_vars_referenced: []              # Env var names read or written during this session (never log values)
    restricted_paths_approached: []      # Any restricted_write_paths that the agent considered touching
    contract_breaks_risk: false          # true if any change may break X-Customer-Id, event payload, or REST shape
```
